stem: Based on the passage and the data in the table, which statement is most accurate about the relationship between water intake and health benefits?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Drinking 8 cups of water daily produced the highest scores across all health measures.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The more water participants drank, the better their health scores became.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Drinking 12 cups of water daily was most beneficial for skin quality.

choices.label: D
choices.text: There was no significant difference between the three water intake levels.

explanation: Choice A is correct because the data clearly shows that the 8 cups per day group achieved the highest scores in all three categories: energy level (8.1), skin quality (7.9), and wellness (8.0). This supports the passage's claim that the relationship between water intake and health is complex, as more water (12 cups) did not produce better results than the moderate amount (8 cups).

distractorRationale.A: This is the correct answer as explained above.
distractorRationale.B: This is incorrect because the data shows that 12 cups per day produced lower scores than 8 cups per day in all categories, demonstrating that more water doesn't always mean better health.
distractorRationale.C: This is incorrect because the 8 cups group had a higher skin quality score (7.9) than the 12 cups group (7.2).
distractorRationale.D: This is incorrect because there are clear and significant differences between the groups, with the 8 cups group performing notably better than both the 4 cups and 12 cups groups.

passage:
Many people believe that drinking more water always leads to better health. However, recent research suggests that the relationship between water intake and health benefits is more complex than previously thought.

A study conducted by Dr. Sarah Martinez at City University tracked 200 adults over six months. Participants were divided into three groups: those who drank 4 cups of water daily, those who drank 8 cups daily, and those who drank 12 cups daily. The researchers measured energy levels, skin quality, and overall wellness scores.

[THIS IS TABLE: Water Intake Study Results]
Daily Water Intake | Average Energy Level (1-10 scale) | Average Skin Quality Score (1-10 scale) | Average Wellness Score (1-10 scale)
4 cups | 6.2 | 5.8 | 6.0
8 cups | 8.1 | 7.9 | 8.0
12 cups | 7.3 | 7.2 | 7.1

passageMetadata.source: Fictional study data for educational purposes
